title: Save and reopen KV caches
tagline: In Pie the KV cache is a named object inside the engine. Save it after turn 1 and reopen it on turn 2 from user code. Only the new message pays prefill; chat history is the cache itself.
question: Two-turn chat. Turn 1: 'What is Python?' Turn 2: 'Name three common uses.'

naive.label: Naive
naive.sublabel: stateless client — every turn re-prefills the full history

events.value: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language used for many kinds of applications.

1. A scripting language
2. A web development tool
3. A data analysis and ML language
prefill 66 tokens — system + turn 1 user + turn 1 assistant + turn 2 user
total prefill 107 tokens  •  about 194 ms wall

pie.label: Pie
pie.sublabel: Context::save() after turn 1, Context::open() before turn 2

events.value: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language used for many kinds of applications.
turn 2  (Context::open, append only the new user message)

1. A scripting language
2. A web development tool
3. A data analysis and ML language
prefill 17 tokens — only the new user message + cue
total prefill 58 tokens  •  about 114 ms wall  •  3.9× less turn-2 prefill

runCommand: pie run --stdout demo-persistent-kv -- --mode both --turn1 "What is Python?" --turn2 "Name three common uses."
